title: To The Lark.
content:
Good speed, for I this day
    Betimes my matins say:
    Because I do
    Begin to woo,
    Sweet-singing lark,
    Be thou the clerk,
    And know thy when
    To say, Amen.
    And if I prove
    Bless'd in my love,
    Then thou shalt be
    High-priest to me,
    At my return,
    To incense burn;
    And so to solemnise
    Love's and my sacrifice.
meaning:
"To The Lark." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
